nonblocking_notes: The gold answer could explicitly note that the isolation specifications apply only to KE-DM3110 models with the integrated short-circuit isolator, as stated on page 15; however, the question itself already scopes to 'el aislador de cortocircuito integrado en el KE-DM3110', so the scoping is preserved.

Existing retriever-augmentation questions cover isolator engagement/reconnection voltages and devices-between-isolators, but none covers these three parameters (active-isolation consumption, leakage current, series impedance), so there is no duplication.

fact_verdicts.notes: Page 15, table 'Isolamento': 'Assorbimento di corrente (isolamento attivo) 1,5 mA', explicitly valid for KE-DM3110 models with integrated isolator.
Page 16, continuation of isolation table: 'Corrente di dispersione 1 mA max.'

nonblocking_notes: The question identifies the product by family description ('dispositivo direccionable de notificación exterior Kidde Excellence') rather than exact model number (KE-AS3115R-WMIP as in the counterpart); the installation sheet's Technische Daten apply to the covered outdoor notification device series, so the question remains answerable, but naming the model would be crisper.

Retriever-augmentation questions on this document cover the isolator's actuated consumption (5 mA), max isolators per loop, devices between isolators and max cable impedance — none asks for the two nominal current ratings, so no duplication.
Existing retriever-augmentation questions cover the LED/buzzer test and the keyboard test (hyq:049082aa..., hyq:29411a3c...), but not the LCD test, so no semantic duplication.
Counterpart marks F04 (exit via F2) as core while this candidate marks it supplementary; classification difference only, no factual disagreement.

fact_verdicts.notes: Page 37: 'Realice una comprobación de LCD para confirmar que la LCD funciona correctamente.'
Page 37, steps 1-2: 'En el Menú principal, seleccione Prueba' → 'Seleccione Prueba de IU y, a continuación, Test LCD.'
Page 37: 'Una secuencias de test se muestran en la pantalla LCD para ayudar a identificar la ubicación de los píxeles defectuosos.'
